pattern: Tally each distinct value's count once, then add a value's full contribution only when its count is a multiple of k.
hints: You need to know how many times each distinct number occurs before you can decide anything.
A value contributes to the answer only when its total count divides evenly by k; otherwise it contributes nothing.
Count frequencies with a map, then for every value whose count mod k is zero, add value times count to a running total.

edge_inputs.description: exactly one value reaches a multiple of k
single element with k=1 always qualifies
k larger than any frequency so nothing qualifies
all identical with count a multiple of k

stress.description: many repeats over a small value range so several counts hit multiples of k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 12) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 6)
    return [nums, k]
